q3zU7Yy5E5s.root_cause_summary: Both tested stair candidates lie in disconnected navmesh components: the original stair at [-1.30898204, 3.5508982] (candidates 9–13) and the new stair at [-1.23952381, 3.67952381] selected under LLM guidance in candidate_14. Candidate_14's DP5 fix ('chair'→'couch') improved parse rate from 4/4 failures to 2/5 (3 calls parsed), and the LLM-guided stair selection shifted to a nearby alternate centroid — which is equally inaccessible (all Reach_stair_centroid: False, disabled 3 times before permanent disable), and dtg worsens slightly (2.679 vs 2.662) while steps increase to 439. DP5_goal_binding_bug_fix is confirmed insufficient as a standalone lever; navmesh disconnection is the irreducible bottleneck across all nine candidates.

floor_step never resets to 0 after entering stair mode in any of the nine candidates — physical stair traversal never occurs regardless of stair centroid, carrot distance, or LLM parse status

Is the couch reachable via same-floor navigation from the starting pose, and if so, would a reachability precheck allow the step budget to be applied productively to same-floor exploration?

qyAac8rV8Zk.root_cause_summary: The downstairs stair lies in a disconnected navmesh component regardless of which centroid is selected: [-1.26861111, -8.18583333] in candidates 9–13 (min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 170/177) and [-1.23053279, -8.26782787] in candidate_14 (min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 180/187/188). Candidate_14 is the definitive harness-layer test: with DP5 goal-binding fixed, all 4 LLM calls parse successfully (dp7_empty=0/4 vs 1/1 failures in candidates 11–13), the agent explores 99 more steps (342 vs 243), yet no convergence occurs and dtg worsens slightly (4.022 vs 3.957), confirming that working LLM guidance does not redirect the agent toward the couch and cannot overcome navmesh disconnection. All harness-layer DP levers are now exhausted.
key_evidence: Candidate_14: steps=342, reinits=1, stair_runs=1, dp7_empty=0/4, dtg=4.022 — all 4 LLM calls parsed (vs 1/1 or 2/2 failures in prior candidates); stair centroid shifted to [-1.23053279, -8.26782787]; min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 180/188/187 with no convergence; 3 stair disable cycles before permanent disable

mL8ThkuaVTM.root_cause_summary: Stair traversal succeeds robustly at step 120 via passive upstairs detection (floor_step resets to 0) in all nine candidates including candidate_14. The toilet room remains geometrically inaccessible from the floor-2 stair landing: floor-2 frontiers exhaust at floor_step=13 in every candidate, 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found' fires at step 133, and all further reinit cycles terminate identically at step 163. Candidate_14's DP3 change triggered one inter-floor LLM call (dp7_empty=0/1, parsed successfully) but dtg worsened slightly (4.210 vs 4.154 in candidates 11–13), providing a ninth data point that working LLM guidance — whether intra-floor or inter-floor — cannot overcome the floor-2 geometry constraint. DP3 is now ruled out. All known harness-layer DP levers are exhausted.
key_evidence: Candidate_14: steps=163, reinits=3, stair_runs=0, dp7_empty=0/1, dtg=4.210 — DP3 change triggered 1 LLM call (vs 0 calls in candidates 11–13); call parsed successfully; dtg worsened from 4.154 to 4.210; trajectory structure (stair success at step 120, floor_step=13 at step 133, terminate at step 163) byte-for-byte identical to candidates 11–13

bxsVRursffK.root_cause_summary: Identical failure mode to mL8ThkuaVTM: after successful stair climbing at step 154 (reinit 1, floor_step resets to 0), a second floor reinit fires at step 167 after exactly 13 steps on the new floor (floor_step resets to 0 with Mode=explore), leaving only 13 exploration steps before the episode terminates at step 180. The 13-step-to-second-reinit timing is identical to mL8ThkuaVTM (step 120→135), confirming a shared hardcoded floor initialization threshold in ascent_policy.py that bypasses all harness DPs. The initial floor consumed 137 steps (floor_step=137 at climb_stair entry), leaving only 26 steps for two-floor exploration — insufficient even without the premature second reinit.

13-step reinit interval matches mL8ThkuaVTM exactly (step 120→135 there, step 154→167 here), strongly implicating a shared hardcoded threshold in ascent_policy.py

DYehNKdT76V.root_cause_summary: Candidate_10's harness change introduced a premature floor switch (reinits=1) within the first ~13 steps, landing the agent on a sparse secondary floor at coordinates [-1.92, -2.52] with only 2 low-score frontiers (0.198@3.2m, 0.176@3.4m) instead of the 6-frontier floor-1 region ([-5.85, 0.65] range) where the chair was successfully found in all prior candidates. With 2 frontiers exhausted by step 50 and no stair traversal (stair_runs=0), the episode terminates with 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found' before the chair is encountered. This is a pure harness-regression: the scene is solvable, confirmed by SPL=0.86 success in every prior candidate.
key_evidence: Candidate_10: only 2 frontiers at [-1.91767767, -2.51767767] with raw scores 0.198@3.2m and 0.176@3.4m (no proximity boost, both >3m) throughout the episode — vs 6 frontiers at [-5.85, 0.65] range with scores 0.096-0.198 and active proximity boosting in all successful candidates (2-4, 8, 9)

Candidate_10: steps=50, stair_runs=0 — vs steps=129, stair_runs=1 in all six prior candidates; stair mode never activates because the sparse secondary floor exposes no stair frontier in its mapped region

Candidate_10: reinits=1 with floor_step=1 at the first logged step — indicates a floor switch fired at or before step 12, before normal frontier mapping could populate the obstacle map, exactly mirroring candidate_1's SDP-C frontier_count==0 bug
Video filename: 'never_saw_target_did_not_travel_stairs_feasible' — stairs ARE navigable at navmesh level; failure is a harness-triggered premature switch, not geometry
dp7_empty=1/1: single LLM call with empty response (fallback index=0); semantic guidance cannot recover from starting on the wrong floor
Candidates 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 all succeed with SPL=0.86 at identical step 109 navigate → step 129 success; DYehNKdT76V failure is strictly a candidate_10 regression

candidate_outcomes.candidate_2: SUCCESS: 129 steps, reinits=1, stair_runs=1 — 6 frontiers at [-5.85, 0.65], active proximity boosting, chair found via stair traversal
candidate_outcomes.candidate_3: SUCCESS: 129 steps, reinits=1, stair_runs=1 — identical 6-frontier exploration pattern with LLM memory augmentation
candidate_outcomes.candidate_4: SUCCESS: 129 steps, reinits=1, stair_runs=1 — equal_weighting value map does not affect floor-1 exploration path
candidate_outcomes.candidate_8: SUCCESS: 129 steps, reinits=2, stair_runs=1 — STOP→MOVE_FORWARD patch has no negative effect on DYehNKdT76V
candidate_outcomes.candidate_9: SUCCESS: 129 steps, reinits=2, stair_runs=1 — passive stair detection at step 91 (floor_step=91); still finds chair on correct floor
candidate_outcomes.candidate_10: FAIL: 50 steps, reinits=1, stair_runs=0 — premature floor switch to 2-frontier secondary floor; chair never encountered; terminates 'no unexplored stairs or frontiers found'

ruled_out_reasoning.DP9: stair_runs=0 confirms the carrot-waypoint stair approach path is never invoked; DP9 is not on the causal path
ruled_out_reasoning.DP1: Low raw scores (0.198, 0.176) are a consequence of being on the wrong floor with few options, not a scoring formula failure; candidates 2-9 show richer raw scores (0.096-0.198) boosted by proximity on the correct floor
ruled_out_reasoning.DP2: LLM fires once (dp7_empty=1/1) but fallback to index=0 selects the only viable frontier anyway; LLM failure is secondary to the wrong-floor starting position
ruled_out_reasoning.DP5: Prior candidates succeed with dp7_empty=5-6/6; the chair is found via frontier scoring not LLM guidance — goal-binding bug is not causally involved in this regression
ruled_out_reasoning.DP4: SSIM deduplication is irrelevant with only 2 frontiers available

open_questions: What specific harness logic in candidate_10 fires the floor switch at floor_step<=12 — is it a frontier_count==0 condition that triggers before the obstacle map is populated, re-introducing the same bug as candidate_1's SDP-C regression?

Is the 2-frontier floor at [-1.92, -2.52] floor 2 (above or below the chair's floor), and would the chair be findable if the agent arrived on it via stair traversal rather than premature harness-triggered switch?
Does candidate_10's explore-mode guard threshold change interact with a coverage-based switch condition that fires at frontier_count==0 before steps_on_floor reaches a safety minimum?
